text:
Can you proof read this paragraph?

> In the UK we are partnering with Levelise. They sell a HEMS. Selling this device requires several additional pieces of hardware. On one side of the fuse box is a regular PV system with panels, inverter (hybrid or not), and a smart meter. On the other side is a DC battery, a AC-coupled inverter, and the Levelise HEMS. There is no way of getting cloud to select all this on one building. But with two buildings this can be done out of the box. Essentially we combine the BuildingSpec from a regular PV spec and a regular standalone battery spec onto one Spec and with no changes to the equipment selector we get all the components we need.


Certainly, here is the corrected version of your paragraph:

---

In the UK, we are partnering with Levelise, which sells a HEMS. Selling this device requires several additional pieces of hardware. On one side of the fuse box is a regular PV system with panels, an inverter (hybrid or not), and a smart meter. On the other side is a DC battery, an AC-coupled inverter, and the Levelise HEMS. There is no way of getting the cloud to select all this for one building, but with two buildings, this can be done out of the box. Essentially, we combine the BuildingSpec from a regular PV spec and a regular standalone battery spec into one Spec. Without any changes to the equipment selector, we get all the components we need.

---

Changes made:

1. Added a comma after "In the UK" for proper punctuation.
2. Changed "They sell a HEMS" to "which sells a HEMS" for clarity.
3. Corrected " There is no way of getting cloud to select all this on one building." to "There is no way of getting the cloud to select all this for one building," for better readability and proper grammar.
4. Changed "a AC-coupled inverter" to "an AC-coupled inverter" to match the correct article usage.
5. Reworded "onto one Spec and with no changes to the equipment selector..." to "into one Spec. Without any changes to the equipment selector..." for better readability and coherence.

If you have any more specific preferences or need further adjustments, please let me know!
summary: Partnering with Levelise for HEMS Integration in the UK
